name: Convox Container PaaS API
what_it_does: Open-source Kubernetes-powered container platform with REST API — enabling development teams to deploy applications to their own AWS, GCP, Azure, or DigitalOcean accounts using a Heroku-like CLI and API workflow, with automatic SSL, load balancing, rolling deploys, and multi-rack (environment) management, abstracting Kubernetes complexity.
use_cases: Deploying containerized applications via Convox API for Heroku-like deployment workflow on own cloud
Managing Convox racks (Kubernetes clusters) via API for multi-environment infrastructure automation
Scaling Convox services and processes via API for automated capacity management
Querying Convox deployment logs and app status via API for deployment monitoring
Managing Convox environment variables and secrets via API for configuration automation
not_for: Teams without AWS/GCP/Azure accounts (Convox deploys to your own cloud, not managed infrastructure)
Fully managed PaaS with no infrastructure ownership (use Railway or Render)
Teams needing enterprise SLA and 24/7 support from the platform
best_when: A dev team wants Heroku-like simplicity for deploying to their own AWS/GCP/Azure Kubernetes — with open-source transparency, no vendor lock-in, and infrastructure-in-own-cloud model.
avoid_when: You don't want infrastructure ownership, need fully managed databases/services, or lack cloud account access.

auth.notes: API key from Convox console. Bearer token auth. Rack-level authentication (each rack has its own API key). Webhook for deployment events. CLI (convox) for local deployment. Open source — self-hosted racks authenticate with their own generated keys. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — rack API key provides full rack access. Console API key for managing multiple racks.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source (self-hosted) is free; Convox Console free for 1 rack
pricing.paid_tiers: Console Pro $20/month per rack; Enterprise custom; infrastructure costs separate (your cloud bills)
pricing.notes: Founded 2015 by former Heroku employees (Noah Zoschke, David Dollar). Open source Apache 2.0 license. Convox Console (cloud management UI) is commercial. Infrastructure costs go to user's cloud account. Multiple cloud provider support: AWS, GCP, Azure, DigitalOcean. Docker Compose-like convox.yml for app definition. Rolling deploys, blue-green deployments. Service mesh with mTLS via Linkerd. Battery-included Kubernetes abstraction.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for rack and app management. API key bearer auth. Good CLI documentation. Webhook for deployment events. Open source — can inspect source for API details. Infrastructure in own cloud — no Convox-managed data. Rate limits not documented. Rack-level API keys require per-rack auth management.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: App and service reads fully idempotent. Deployment creation requires idempotency at CI layer. Environment variable sets are idempotent by key. Rack reads idempotent by name.
known_agent_gotchas: Each Convox rack has its own API key — multi-rack agents need key management per rack
Infrastructure runs in user's cloud — AWS/GCP/Azure billing separate from Convox Console fee
convox.yml (not Dockerfile alone) defines services — app definition is required
Rate limits not documented — test before high-frequency API calls
Open source version vs Console version — some features only in Console

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. User-controlled data residency (infrastructure in own cloud). API key bearer auth. Open source — auditable code. Rack-level API keys with no cross-rack access. Environment secrets encrypted at rest in Kubernetes secrets. mTLS between services via Linkerd option. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Compliance depends on user's own cloud configuration and certifications.
